attribution.text: Route, junction, and history details adapted from the Wikipedia article "Talimena Scenic Drive", by Wikipedia contributors, licensed under CC BY-SA 4.0.

sources.name: Federal Highway Administration — National Scenic Byways

paragraphs: The current route opened in 1969 and formed a stretch of what would become Oklahoma State Highway 1. It was dedicated on June 7, 1970, by Lucy Baines Johnson-Nugent, the daughter of U.S. President Lyndon B. Johnson.

The roadway was designated as a National Forest Scenic Byway on February 8, 1989. It was later made an Arkansas State Scenic Byway on January 7, 1998, and an Oklahoma State Scenic Byway on October 10, 2002. The National Scenic Byway status was conferred on September 22, 2005.
